content.full_text:
March 22, 1991

Mark Morodomi, Esq.
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Morodomi:

Our Director, Charles Uznay, attended the Association of California Water Agencies "Board Members' Roles and Responsibilities" seminar in Burlingame on February 8, 1991 at which you spoke on the Conflict of Interest Law.

Mr. Uznay questioned you regarding the four questions on page 18 of the "A Guide to the Political Reform Act of 1974 - California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public Officials."

You stated that all four of the questions must be answered yes for the official to be disqualified from a decision because of a conflict of interest.

Mr. Uznay's answers to questions number 1, 2, and 3 are yes and number 4 is no. Your answer to him was that he would not be disqualified from a decision because of a conflict of interest if these are his answers.

We are asking that you confirm in writing that our understanding that Mr. Uznay would not have a conflict if his answers to the questions are as stated in the paragraph above is correct.

If you have any questions or comments, please call.

Very truly yours,

BYRON-BETHANY IRRIGATION DISTRICT

[signature]
Lois Stornetta, Secretary

cc: Charles Uznay
